decks.notes: This deck focuses on using disruptive cards like Thoughtseize and Wasteland to control the opponent's resources while applying pressure with efficient creatures like Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage and selection, while Flow State and Orcish Bowmasters help maintain tempo by disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes counterspells like Force of Will and Daze to protect its threats. By combining disruption, card advantage, and aggressive threats, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure to win the game quickly.

The deck focuses on using Cloudpost and Tron lands to ramp up mana quickly, allowing for the casting of powerful spells and abilities to control the game. It includes a mix of counterspells, removal, card draw, and win conditions such as Ugin, Karn, and Kozilek's Command. The goal is to establish a strong board presence while disrupting the opponent's game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with powerful threats and staying ahead in the resource game. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents through superior mana generation and efficient spell choices.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to generate mana and control the board while applying pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures. It includes powerful artifacts such as Mox Opal and Lotus Petal to accelerate its game plan, along with disruptive cards like Force of Will and Chalice of the Void to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Emry, Lurker of the Loch allows for recursion of artifacts from the graveyard, providing additional value. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent quickly with a combination of fast mana and efficient threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupt their game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void. The abundance of mana acceleration sources allows for explosive starts and the aggressive creatures like Glaring Fleshraker and Eldrazi Linebreaker put pressure on the opponent early on. Thought-Knot Seer provides disruption by taking away key cards from the opponent's hand. The deck aims to quickly establish board presence and close out the game before the opponent can mount a defense.

The deck focuses on using Gaea's Cradle and mana accelerators like Birds of Paradise and Ignoble Hierarch to generate a large amount of mana quickly. It utilizes control elements like Thoughtseize and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also including threats like Wight of the Reliquary and Craterhoof Behemoth to close out the game. The deck also has access to powerful tutors like Green Sun's Zenith and Natural Order to find key creatures like Endurance and Grist, the Hunger Tide. Overall, the deck aims to efficiently ramp up mana and disrupt the opponent's strategy while setting up for a powerful finish.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing lands to disrupt the opponent's resources and strategies. By using cards like Dark Depths, Wasteland, and Ghost Quarter to disrupt the opponent's mana base and key lands, the deck aims to slow down the opponent's game plan while building up its own key lands like Thespian's Stage and Urza's Saga. With cards like Exploration and Life from the Loam providing additional land advantage, the deck can outvalue the opponent and eventually win the game with powerful land-based win conditions.

The main focus of the deck is to use The Fantasticar card in combination with other cards such as Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate a large amount of mana quickly. This allows the player to cast powerful spells like Murktide Regent and Brazen Borrower early in the game. The deck also includes a lot of card draw and control spells to ensure consistency and protection while executing the combo. The overall strategy is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures and disruptive spells.

The focus of this deck is to control the game through a mix of counter spells, removal spells, and disruptive abilities. It aims to disrupt the opponent's plays with cards like Stifle, Wasteland, and Daze, while also utilizing powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi to generate card advantage and maintain control over the battlefield. Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd provides a powerful win condition, and the deck also includes cards like Swords to Plowshares and Force of Will for additional control and protection. The deck is designed to outmaneuver and outlast the opponent, ultimately emerging victorious through careful strategic play.

The main concept of this deck is to use artifacts and combo pieces to quickly assemble The Fantasticar, which can generate a lot of value and potentially win the game on its own. The deck has a heavy focus on fast mana and card draw to fuel the combo and disrupt opponents with counterspells. Emry, Lurker of the Loch serves as a key piece in recurring artifacts and enabling the combo. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Boomerang Basics and Sink into Stupor to buy time and control the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing artifact synergy and ramp to assemble key pieces for a powerful combo using The Fantasticar. Emry, Lurker of the Loch helps with recursion while Pinnacle Emissary provides additional card advantage. Urza's Saga and Mishra's Bauble help to search for essential pieces, while Chalice of the Void and Force of Will provide disruption and protection. The deck aims to control the board with cards like Sink into Stupor and Kappa Cannoneer until it can combo off and win the game.

This deck focuses on assembling a powerful combo involving The Fantasticar, Glaring Fleshraker, and Planar Nexus to generate a powerful board presence quickly. The key to the deck is using mana acceleration like Urza's Workshop and Grim Monolith to ramp into the combo pieces, while controlling the board with cards like Chalice of the Void and Trinisphere. The inclusion of cards like The One Ring and Mystic Forge provide additional utility and card advantage to keep the engine running smoothly.

This deck aims to control the game by utilizing lands as a resource. The key strategy involves using cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage to create powerful token creatures, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Wasteland and Bojuka Bog. The deck also has a strong recursion engine with Life from the Loam to ensure a steady stream of resources. By managing the battlefield with lands and controlling the opponent's resources, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent to secure victory.

The deck consists of a combo strategy focused on cheating powerful creatures, such as Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, into play quickly using cards like Show and Tell, Sneak Attack, and Omniscience. It also utilizes cards like Lotus Petal and City of Traitors to accelerate mana production, while including counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce for protection. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with its high-impact threats early in the game, with the potential to win in just a few turns if left unchecked.

The main focus of this deck is to establish a strong mana base using Cloudpost and Tron lands in order to ramp up quickly to powerful threats like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Karn, the Great Creator. The deck also features a control aspect with cards like Force of Negation and Force of Will to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Stock Up and Eldrazi Confluence for card advantage and removal, further solidifying its control strategy. Ultimately, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful spells and threats while controlling the game.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using a variety of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi to generate card advantage and provide additional control options. The mana base is carefully constructed to allow for flexibility in casting spells while also disrupting the opponent's mana with Wasteland. The goal is to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent until the control player can secure the win.

decks.player: Joao Victor De Oliveira Miranda E Silva
decks.notes: The deck focuses on generating mana quickly with cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to cast Doomsday and set up a winning sequence using cards like Thassa's Oracle and The Fantasticar. It also includes protection spells like Force of Will and Daze to help ensure the combo can be executed successfully. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and win the game in a fast and efficient manner, making it a high-risk, high-reward strategy in Legacy 1 vs 1 format.
